channel.title: smol-eggo's Game Journal
channel.description: Personal game reviews, handheld impressions, and Retroid Pocket 6 compatibility notes.

item.title: 7 Days to Die
item.description: Until Easy Anti Cheat support changes, this one is staying on my desktop.

item.title: Rogue Legacy 2
item.description: If the heir selection bug gets fixed, this would be close to a perfect handheld experience. Even with that quirk, Rogue Legacy 2 is an absolute joy to play…

item.title: Alice: Madness Returns
item.description: If someone told me the menus were running on a different device, I'd probably believe them. 😄 Thankfully, that's where the weirdness ends. The actual game…

item.title: Animal Well
item.description: This feels like the definitive way for me to play Animal Well. The combination of flawless performance, beautiful visuals, and the RP6's form factor finally…

item.title: Diablo II: Resurrected
item.description: Not every game is ready for the journey. For now, Sanctuary will have to wait.

item.title: Gunfire Reborn
item.description: Not every compatibility test ends with a dramatic crash. Sometimes a game simply refuses to move past the starting line. Until a future Proton update or…

item.title: Stubbs the Zombie in Rebel Without a Pulse
item.description: Sometimes revisiting an old favourite is disappointing. Stubbs the Zombie isn't one of those games. It still has that weird charm, it's still a blast to…

item.title: Another Crab's Treasure
item.description: Playable isn't always enjoyable. For a slower RPG I'd happily accept 30 FPS, but a Soulslike asks a little more from both the player and the hardware.

item.title: Atlyss
item.description: This one feels close. I can see the finish line, but we're not quite there yet.

item.title: Bugsnax
item.description: 🎵 Talkin' 'bout Bugsnax... 🎵 Sorry. Once it's in your head, it's there for the rest of the day.

item.title: CloverPit
item.description: LET'S GO GAMBLING! ...and just like the game itself, I kept telling myself "one more spin."

item.title: Crisis Core: Final Fantasy VII Reunion
item.description: A game I really wanted to recommend, but not one I'd choose to play on the RP6.

item.title: Cuphead
item.description: The hardware passed the test. I'm still working on passing mine.

item.title: Date Everything!
item.description: There aren't many games where dating your toaster somehow feels more natural on a handheld. This is one of them.

item.title: Dead Format
item.description: Sometimes the problem isn't low frame rates. Sometimes the game simply refuses to stay alive long enough to measure them.

item.title: Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth
item.description: If you asked me to name one JRPG that perfectly matches the RP6, Cyber Sleuth would be near the top of the list. This feels less like making a compromise…

item.title: Dragon Quest XI S: Echoes of an Elusive Age
item.description: Sometimes the best tweak is patience. Rather than spending hours chasing the perfect settings, I've decided to wait for the upcoming Switch 2 version and…

item.title: Everhood
item.description: The best compatibility tests are the ones that accidentally become gaming sessions. Everhood passed that test with flying colours.

item.title: Far Cry 3
item.description: Don't judge this one by its opening minutes. Once you're free to explore, Far Cry 3 becomes a surprisingly enjoyable adventure on the RP6.

item.title: Final Fantasy X HD Remaster
item.description: One of those games that simply belongs on a handheld.
